title: How can you retrieve data from a one-to-many relationship in Laravel?
description: How can you retrieve data from a one-to-many relationship in Laravel?
alt: How can you retrieve data from a one-to-many relationship in Laravel?

children.value:
We saw how data is saved in multiple different ways within the database using the One-To-Many relationship in Laravel.
But how to fetch data from the database?
There are several ways in which data can be fetched from a database.
These methods are divided into two basic methods:
This method is divided into two sub-methods depending on how the data is organized after it is fetched from the data base
file so that we can test these methods and make the following modifications.
Here we can choose the columns for each relationship as follows.
In order to know the difference between selecting or not selecting columns from the database, let us see the specific response to each case.
In the first case, with all data retrieved without exception, we find that

We will create an API Resource for the Post form by executing this command in the command prompt.

props.code: php artisan make:resource PostResource

children.value: You go to the following path
and we begin to modify each of the following:

props.code:
<?php

namespace App\Http\Resources;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Http\Resources\Json\JsonResource;

class PostResource extends JsonResource
{
    public function toArray(Request $request): array
    {
        return [
            'id' => $this->id,
            'title' => $this->title,
            'body' => $this->body,
        ];
    }
}

You go to the following path
and we start editing the file

props.code:
<?php

namespace App\Http\Resources;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Http\Resources\Json\JsonResource;

class PostResource extends JsonResource
{
    public function toArray(Request $request): array
    {
        return [
            'id' => $this->id,
            'title' => $this->title,
            'body' => $this->body,
            'user' => UserResource::make($this->whenLoaded('user')),
        ];
    }
}
